body.title: [sprint] DickSimnel front-door bring-up hardening: is_blocked() gate + 'coming' ack to orchestrator
body.intention: I intend that when the DickSimnel front-door is asked to bring DS.0 up, it first checks the shim's is_blocked() gate and, on spawn, reports 'coming' back to the orchestrator — so a blocked device is never spawned and Granny knows a worker is en route rather than guessing.
body.description:
PHASE 1 (of D-granny-pull-dispatch-model). Reconciliation verdict PARTIAL: the front-door launches DS.0 on a bus-wake (frontdoor.py:108-109 idle_wait -> _ensure_device_awake -> _spawn_device frontdoor.py:164-204), but it (a) never consults shim.is_blocked() (which EXISTS at shim.py:79-81) before spawning, and (b) sends no 'coming' acknowledgement back to the orchestrator. Add both: gate the spawn on not-blocked, and emit a lightweight 'device coming' bus reply to the waker so Granny can distinguish 'launching' from 'no worker'.

**Affected files:** unseen_university/devices/dicksimnel/frontdoor.py (spawn path), possibly unseen_university/devices/dicksimnel/shim.py (expose is_blocked to the front-door).
**Design rules:** log-every-interface-crossing (the 'coming' ack is a bus crossing — log at INFO); no-sqlite (n/a).
**Scope boundary:** IN — is_blocked() pre-spawn gate + 'coming' ack on spawn. OUT — the pull/poll redesign (that is T-converge-worker-dispatch), class-encoded semaphore (T-availability-semaphore-build-class), or changing what wakes the front-door.
**Test plan:** unit — front-door with a blocked shim does NOT spawn (red: spawns anyway; green: no subprocess, logs blocked); front-door on wake with an unblocked shim emits a 'coming' bus message (red: silent; green: ack present).
**Completion criteria:** front-door consults is_blocked() before every spawn and emits a 'coming' ack; both verified by test.
**Rollback:** git revert — front-door returns to unconditional spawn-on-wake.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)

[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)

[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
